facts.text: Holliswood got its name from developer William Dunton, who honored his New Hampshire hometown and first marketed the area as an upscale subdivision in the early 1900s.
The neighborhood drew professionals and physicians who valued the quiet hills while commuting to Manhattan via Hillside Avenue and the nearby Long Island Rail Road’s Hollis Station.
A Queens Public Library branch serves Holliswood residents from its building on 202‑05 Hillside Avenue, offering a quiet spot for reading and community events.
In 2002 the Holliswood Civic Association became the first in New York City to successfully petition for larger 60‑by‑100 lot sizes, reshaping the neighborhood’s residential character.
Holliswood sits on the Harbor Hill Moraine, an ancient glacial ridge, and is bordered by Jamaica Estates, Cunningham Park, Hollis, and Queens Village.
